hero.headline: Tell us what you want to build.
hero.placeholder: Describe what you want to build...
hero.notSure: Not sure where to start? Try one of these

suggestions.label: Reservation platform
suggestions.prompt: Build a reservation platform for my business — customers can book appointments, manage availability, and receive confirmation emails.

suggestions.label: Operations dashboard
suggestions.prompt: Create an operations dashboard for my team — centralize tasks, KPIs, and team activity in one internal tool.

suggestions.label: AI customer support
suggestions.prompt: I need an AI assistant for customer support — it should answer FAQs, escalate complex issues, and integrate with our existing chat.

suggestions.label: Custom workflow tool
suggestions.prompt: Build custom software to automate my business workflow — reduce manual steps and connect my existing tools.

suggestions.label: Mobile app
suggestions.prompt: Create a mobile app for my business — available on iOS and Android with a clean, modern design.

suggestions.label: E-commerce store
suggestions.prompt: I need an e-commerce store for my products — with catalog management, cart, checkout, and order tracking.

suggestions.label: Client portal
suggestions.prompt: Build a client portal where my customers can log in, view their projects, upload documents, and communicate with my team.

suggestions.label: Internal tool
suggestions.prompt: Create an internal tool for my team to manage operations — track tasks, approvals, and performance metrics in one place.

footer.tagline: Custom software built in real code. From idea to production-ready applications, powered by AI.

hero.headline: A code-first company
hero.description: Noon turns ambiguous software demand into concrete scope and delivers it in code. Execution quality, product judgment, AI-assisted leverage.

thesis.headline: Why the model exists
thesis.mainDescription: Noon is built for situations where the need is real but the software shape is still unclear. The job is to turn that ambiguity into scoped, production-minded execution.
thesis.clearProcessDescription: From initial conversation to deployment, every step is transparent.
thesis.aiAcceleratedDescription: Maxwell handles scoping, and our tools accelerate development without sacrificing quality.
thesis.enterpriseReadyDescription: Built for scale, security, and long-term maintainability.
thesis.codeOwnershipDescription: Ownership depends on the engagement model, while client data remains the client's.

operatingModel.headline: How we work

principles.title: Start from the problem
principles.description: Most valuable conversations start with operational friction, not a stack wishlist.

principles.title: Translate to a real build path
principles.description: The path becomes explicit enough to scope and deliver: internal system, product, workflow, or custom architecture.

principles.title: Execute with AI as leverage
principles.description: AI compresses repetitive work but does not remove the need for architecture or product judgment.

notNoon: No-code shortcuts that collapse when logic gets serious
Generic marketplace for every kind of interest
Product theater hiding weak delivery behind polish

metrics.label: Code-first delivery
metrics.description: Every project ships as production code

metrics.label: Scope direction
metrics.description: The first direction is defined before build work starts

metrics.label: AI-assisted workflows
metrics.description: Tooling accelerates execution without replacing judgment

items.title: Scope before execution
items.description: Direction is defined and agreed before production code is written. Ambiguity is resolved at the start, not during development.

items.title: Working software, not documentation
items.description: Every project ends with deployed, runnable code — not specs, wireframes, or prototypes delivered as a final product.

items.title: Explicit exclusions
items.description: What is not included is clearly stated. Vague scope is the main cause of misaligned expectations; we eliminate it by design.

items.title: Judgment, not blind execution
items.description: The right question is what should be built, not just what was asked for. Product judgment is part of every project.

stack.headline: Technology we use
stack.description: Technology choices follow the product, not the other way around.

cta.headline: Ready to build?
cta.description: Start a conversation with Maxwell or reach out directly.

hero.headline: What we build
hero.description: Noon delivers production-grade software across a wide range of product types. The right choice depends on the problem, not the category.

serviceTypes.title: AI & Automation
serviceTypes.description: Intelligent assistants, workflow automation, and AI-powered tooling for teams that need speed without losing operational control.

serviceTypes.title: Web Solutions
serviceTypes.description: From customer-facing experiences to internal platforms, built as real software with production-grade architecture.

serviceTypes.title: Mobile Solutions
serviceTypes.description: Native and cross-platform mobile applications focused on clear flows and operational reliability.

serviceTypes.title: Custom Software
serviceTypes.description: Software shaped around your internal logic and non-standard workflows when generic systems stop being useful.

items.problem: Manual work that should be automated
items.summary: AI-assisted workflows for teams losing time to repetitive work and human bottlenecks.

items.problem: Operations that need one central system
items.summary: Dashboards and portals that consolidate data, workflows, and reporting into one surface.

items.problem: A product that needs to launch as real software
items.summary: Production-minded builds for founders who understand the problem and need something real.

items.problem: Workflows that generic tools don't fit
items.summary: Custom software built around business logic that breaks inside generic tools.

steps.title: Clarify the constraint
steps.description: Isolate the business problem and the bottleneck that justifies software work.
steps.output: A clear problem statement and the bottleneck worth solving in software.

steps.title: Translate to software path
steps.description: Choose the right shape: workflow system, platform, product, or custom architecture.
steps.output: A recommended software direction matched to the actual operating need.

steps.title: Move into scoped delivery
steps.description: Turn direction into proposal, build scope, and delivery plan.
steps.output: Proposal, build scope, and delivery plan ready for execution.

cta.headline: Ready to scope your project?
cta.description: Start with Maxwell to clarify the problem, or reach out directly.

hero.headline: Pre-scoped builds
hero.description: A starting point for common software types. Each template maps to a clear delivery scope.

cta.headline: Don't see what you need?
cta.description: Start with Maxwell and describe what you want to build.

hero.headline: Contact Noon
hero.description: Tell us what you need, and we'll route it correctly. For project requests, we usually review the inquiry first and then continue with the right next step.
hero.responseTime: We usually respond within 1-2 business days.

inquiryPaths.title: Project request
inquiryPaths.description: Scope, propose, or build custom software.

inquiryPaths.title: General question
inquiryPaths.description: Company, legal, or broader questions about Noon.

cta.headline: Need a first direction before you reach out?
cta.description: Let Maxwell help structure the initial request before Noon reviews it.
